_purpose: Snapshot of figures used in docs/baseline_thresholds.md that were derived from live Prometheus queries during this session and don't otherwise exist in any committed ground-truth JSON. Committed so these claims are reproducible from the repo alone, without re-querying a live Prometheus instance.

executor_oom.cgroup_memory_limit_note: 1152Mi (mebibytes) = 1152 * 1024 * 1024 bytes exactly, from infra/spark/submit-pod.yaml's spark.executor.memory=768m + spark.kubernetes.memoryOverheadFactor=0.4 computation
executor_oom.organic_settled_baseline_note: Corrected from an earlier remembered figure of 395.7-525.2MB during this snapshot -- direct re-query found ramptest7's real settled value was 391.9MB, below the previously-cited floor. Range is per-pod, per-rep container_memory_working_set_bytes{container="spark-kubernetes-executor"} queried over the 90s immediately before each rep's injection_timestamp_utc, restricted to reps where the pod had genuinely settled (not still cold-starting from the previous rep's OOM-kill replacement -- ramptest4/ramptest8's pre-injection windows only caught cold-start values near 0MB and are excluded from this range as non-representative).
executor_oom.ramptest6_note: Full injection-to-OOM real Prometheus sample sequence for one rep, showing the genuine 4-point rising signal referenced in docs/research_context.md Section 6.2 and docs/baseline_thresholds.md Section 2. Queried 2026-07-11T17:26:00Z-17:30:20Z, step=15s, deduplicated for distinct values.

steady_state_noise_floor_check.note: Queried during the executor_oom ramp campaign, a window with zero disk_pressure fault activity -- genuine non-fault steady-state, not a synthetic estimate. 207MB max natural fluctuation over 67 real minutes, vs. the ~3.22GB real injected drop -- roughly 15x separation.

real_per_rep_max_scrape_duration_seconds.note: The true maximum scrape_duration_seconds{job="kafka-broker-jmx"} observed in Prometheus's full stored time series per rep's [injection_timestamp_utc, target_recovered_utc] window -- NOT the fault script's own self-reported peak_scrape_duration_seconds_during_fault field. The script's field is computed by sampling 3x in quick succession right after degraded_detected_ts fires (fault_injection/network_degradation.py line ~140), which can miss a later, higher real scrape that Prometheus's own periodic scraping goes on to capture. Found to differ in 3 of 8 reps (campaign2, campaign5, campaign6) -- the script's self-reported peak understated the true scraped maximum in each case.
